channel.title: AATIP on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in AATIP on Alien Truth Social

item.title: New Emails Reveal Pentagon Effort to Align Messaging on AATIP and Luis Elizondo
item.description:
Newly released Pentagon emails from May 2019 reveal an internal contradiction regarding Luis Elizondo’s role in the Advanced Aerospace Threat Identification Program (AATIP). A senior Pentagon official, Neill Tipton, stated in an email that Elizondo had no assigned responsibilities related to AATIP during his time under Tipton. However, other emails show Tipton was directly communicating with Elizondo about AATIP and reviewing a memo concerning responsibilities for it. Pentagon officials expressed concern about these contradictions and the need for consistent messaging. Despite Tipton’s 2019 statement, previously released records show him engaging in direct discussions with Elizondo about AATIP responsibilities in 2017. Luis Elizondo disputes Tipton’s characterization, stating that documentary evidence shows Tipton was aware of his involvement and agreed to assume his responsibilities.

Source: https://www.theblackvault.com/documentarchive/new-emails-reveal-pentagon-effort-to-align-messaging-on-aatip-and-luis-elizondo

item.title: AATIP Resurfaces in 2022 TS-SCI Briefing, According to Navy FOIA Release
item.description:
Two Freedom of Information Act requests to the Department of the Navy yielded a single responsive record: an email chain detailing a March 2022 briefing on the Advance Aerospace Threat Identification Program (AATIP) and the Airborne Object Identification and Management Synchronization Group (AOIMSG). This briefing, held at Roosevelt Hall on Fort McNair, included classified TS-SCI sessions and discussed topics such as congressional reports and “Tic-Tac” incidents. Despite redactions, the record’s responsiveness to requests specifically seeking information involving Brennan P. McKernan and Luis Elizondo suggests their names were present in the communications. The reappearance of AATIP in a 2022 briefing, alongside its successor AOIMSG, complicates previous characterizations of the program’s scope and Elizondo’s role.

Source: https://www.theblackvault.com/documentarchive/aatip-resurfaces-in-2022-ts-sci-briefing-according-to-navy-foia-release

item.title: Six and a Half Years Later, the DoD’s Reply to Harry Reid’s AATIP Memo Remains Missing
item.description:
In June 2009, Senator Harry Reid requested heightened protection for the Advanced Aerospace Threat Identification Program (AATIP) in a letter to the Department of Defense. Despite the Department of Defense acknowledging the authenticity of this letter and stating it had responded, a formal response has not been publicly released. This absence of a documented reply conflicts with Pentagon statements and established procedures for handling congressional correspondence. The ongoing dispute over the existence and nature of AATIP, coupled with the difficulty in locating related documents, contributes to the mystery surrounding the program.

Source: https://www.theblackvault.com/documentarchive/six-and-a-half-years-later-the-dods-reply-to-harry-reids-aatip-memo-remains-missing
